pdf.text: Assignment 3: Build Your Data Pipeline Duc Nguyen - 002325579 INFO7375 - Branding & AI Project: Reliable Public Signal Pipeline for Madison I. Project and Problem ● My Madison project is a reliable public-signal ingest layer for downstream agents. Madison's intelligence, content, and research agents need fresh public signals about AI agents, automation, scraping, and AI infrastructure. The problem is that every public source has a different schema, date format, engagement field, and URL pattern. If every downstream agent handles that cleanup separately, the system becomes fragile and hard to reuse. ● This workflow collects data once, normalizes it into a shared schema, removes duplicates, validates required fields, and saves a reusable CSV for Assignment 4. II. Data inventory Source 1: Hacker News via Algolia API ● Type: Public developer discussion and news posts. ● Amount: 60 records. ● Purpose: Capture developer discussion around AI agents and automation. ● Collection method: n8n HTTP Request to the public Hacker News Algolia API. ● Quality: 60/60 complete records. Source 2: DEV Community API ● Type: Developer articles and technical discussions. ● Amount: 60 records. ● Purpose: Capture technical posts about AI, including reactions and comments. ● Collection method: n8n HTTP Request to the public DEV Community API. ● Quality: 60/60 complete records. Source 3: GitHub Repository Search API ● Type: Open-source repository adoption signal. ● Amount: 60 records. ● Purpose: Capture active AI-agent repositories and developer attention signals. ● Collection method: n8n HTTP Request to GitHub REST Search API. ● Quality: 60/60 complete records. Statistics ● 180/180 totals records ● 180 clean records, no corruption ● 3 news sources ● The CSV contains 180 data records plus one quality-summary row generated by the workflow. The required fields were record _ id , source _ name , title , url , and posted_at . All 180 data records contain these required fields. Normalized Schema Column Description record_id Source-specific unique identifier source_name One of hacker_news , dev_to , or github source_type Discussion, developer article, or developer repository title Post, article, or repository title text Description or body snippet when available author Username, article author, or repository owner url Public URL for the record posted_at Standardized source date in YYYY-MM-DD format engagement_score Comparable source-specific engagement score comments_count Comments when available, otherwise zero tags Topic tags or programming language content_hash Stable source-title-URL fingerprint for deduplication collected_at Date the n8n workflow collected the record quality_status complete after validation Workflow Overview The n8n workflow starts with a Manual Trigger, calls three public APIs, normalizes each source into the same schema, appends the branches, validates required fields, removes duplicate source IDs, converts the records to CSV, and writes the final file to disk. 1. Manual Trigger: Starts the repeatable demo run. 2. HTTP - Hacker News: Pulls public posts from the Hacker News Algolia API. 3. HTTP - DEV Community: Pulls public developer articles from DEV. 4. HTTP - GitHub Search: Pulls active AI-agent repository records from GitHub. 5. Normalize nodes: Map each source into the common Madison schema. 6. Merge nodes: Append all source records into one stream. 7. Clean, Deduplicate, Validate: Remove bad records, standardize dates, and mark quality. 8. Convert to CSV: Convert normalized JSON records into a CSV file. 9. Write CSV to Downloads: Save the final output file locally. Setup Guide 1. Install Node.js if it is not already installed. 2. Install n8n with npm install -g n8n. 3. Start n8n with n8n start. 4. Open http://localhost:5678 in a browser. 5. Import or open Nguyen_Duc_A3_Workflow.json. 6. Click Execute workflow. 7. After the nodes turn green, open Nguyen_Duc_A3_Public_Signal_Data.csv. Password and Access Code No passwords or access codes are required for Hacker News or DEV. GitHub can be used without authentication for this small dataset. If GitHub rate limits are reached, an optional GitHub token can be added locally, but the token should never be submitted in Canvas or included in screenshots. Common Errors and Fixes Error Fix Github rate limit Wait and rerun later, or add a local GitHub token. Do not share the token Empty source response Rerun the workflow. The cleanup step still allows other sources to continue CSV missing expected columns Check the normalization Code nodes and rerun the workflow III. Data Quality Controls ● Required-field validation checks record_id , source_name , title , url , and posted_at ● Dates are standardized to YYYY-MM-DD ● Duplicate records are removed using source_name:record_id ● Each completed record is marked with quality_status = complete ● The workflow adds a quality-summary row to document the final count IV. Demo Screenshot 1. Full n8n workflow overview. 2. Hacker News HTTP Request configuration This HTTP Request node uses a public GET request to the Hacker News Algolia API to collect AI-agent discussion records. No authentication or API key is required, and the response is returned as JSON for normalization 3. DEV Community HTTP Request configuration This HTTP Request node collects public developer articles from the DEV Community API using a GET request. The API does not require authentication, and the JSON response is normalized into the shared Madison schema 4. GitHub Search HTTP Request configuration with headers This HTTP Request node collects public AI-agent repository signals from GitHub Search. The request uses public GitHub API headers only, with no token or secret, and returns repository metadata such as name, owner, URL, stars, forks, and update date 5. Cleanup/deduplication Code node 6. Successful green workflow execution The full n8n workflow completed successfully 7. Final CSV

pdf.text: INFO7375 · BRANDING & AI · ASSIGNMENT 02 · PART 1 — TARGET ROLE Pick a lane. Founder-stage AI infra. High impact > prestige. One deep dive, two alternates. TARGET ROLE Software Engineer (Distributed Systems) Browserbase — San Francisco 11–50 · CRV-backed · founded 2024 "Building the platform every agent will depend on." POSTING ziprecruiter.com/c/Browserbase/Job/ Software-Engineer-(Distributed-Systems) REQUIREMENTS Top 3 they list — verbatim. 01 Distributed systems @ scale "Designing, building, and running a large, complex distributed system, at scale on the order of hundreds or thousands of instances." 02 Go or TypeScript "Strong expertise coding in Go or Typescript." 03 Streaming + observability "Streaming-based architecture… observability, monitoring, alerting, and logging." WHY THIS ROLE Same problem. One layer up. Browserbase is the runtime every AI agent will depend on. My Madison contribution solves the same reliability problem one layer up: rate- limit, retry, normalize across noisy upstreams. Backend reliability is my 5/5 pillar. Founding-stage scope is the lane I'm aimed at in the next 6–12 months. Hamming AI (YC S24): 1M+ calls / mo · 90% infra cut · $1M+ / yr saved Viggle (a16z) · Hamming AI (YC S24, top 1% velocity) EXCELLENCE · WHO RUNS ENG WALKER GRIGGS Technical Lead, Browserbase ex-Mux (video infra). Likely signer on distributed-systems hires. PAUL KLEIN IV Solo Founder + CEO ex-Twilio (IPO era), ex-CTO StreamClub (acq'd by Mux 2021). "500 rejections → $300M company." TEAM SHAPE Founding team is tiny. Distributed-systems hires sit one Slack DM from the founders. THE LANE · FOUNDING ENG @ AI INFRA Why high-impact > prestige. 3 postings tracked. Browserbase is the deep dive — Exa and Firecrawl share the archetype. ★ PRIMARY Browserbase SE · Distributed Systems Backend-reliability pillar — direct map. ALT 02 Exa Founding / Backend Engineer Web search-for-AI; Madison feeds it. ALT 03 Firecrawl Founding Engineer OSS scraping for LLMs; literal match. COMMON → AI-infra layer · founder-stage · backend-reliability core · high-impact > prestige INFO7375 · BRANDING & AI · ASSIGNMENT 02 · PART 2 — GAP ANALYSIS Five gaps. One pipeline closes them. Mapped against the Browserbase Distributed Systems posting — verbatim. THEY WANT I HAVE GAP MADISON CLOSES IT 01 Distributed systems at "hundreds or thousands of instances" — at scale. [ verbatim ] Hamming AI (YC S24): scaled distributed testing to 1M+ calls / month. 90% cost cut — internal. Production evidence at horizontal scale, under real adversarial load. → Scale Madison worker pool from 10 → 200+ on Fly / K8s. Publish p50/p99 latency + throughput write-up. 02 Go or TypeScript "Strong expertise coding in Go or Typescript." [ verbatim ] Go @ WEALLNET: microservices REST APIs. Cykod.AI: gRPC + WS. TS @ Ducsigr (solo OSS). Public, OSS-quality Go showing idiomatic concurrency — not just internal repos. → Ship Madison as a public Go repo: errgroup, context, channel backpressure. 90-sec readable README. 03 Streaming-based architecture "Experience in streaming- based architecture." [ verbatim ] Temporal @ Hamming AI. Redis @ WEALLNET (83% API speedup). gRPC stream @ A real streaming bus producers and consumers can hammer. → Replace batch output with Redis Streams (NATS as stretch). Per-platform → canonical → consumer groups.Cykod.AI. 04 Observability + alerting "Observability, monitoring, alerting, and logging." [ verbatim ] Ducsigr (solo OSS): OpenTelemetry + AI root-cause. K8s HA: Prometheus + Grafana. Open, end-to-end o11y on a system I shipped publicly (not behind a SOC2 wall). → Structured logs + ts diff in event payload. Surface via n8n exec history. Grafana Cloud later (stretch). 05 Their actual stack: Stagehand SDK (TS, OSS), Director, Chrome a11y tree, K8s/Docker, multi-region. [ researched ] K8s HA cluster (3+3+LB) via Terraform. Docker, GitHub Actions. A product-fit demo on Browserbase's own product surface. → LinkedIn scraper falls back to Stagehand on anti-bot. Turning their product into Madison's resilience layer.No Stagehand exposure. STACK SOURCED Browserbase blog (Stagehand v3 · Director · Functions · Optimize Web Automations) · public Ashby posting · May 2026 INFO7375 · BRANDING & AI · ASSIGNMENT 02 · PART 3 — PRD Reliable Social Scraping for Madison. v0.1 · ducnguyen6721 · 2026-05-22 01 PROBLEM Stale signal kills downstream agents. Madison's downstream agents (Intelligence, Content, Research) react to social signals. Every scraper fights rate limits, anti-bot, and silent schema drift. Most fail half the time and never report it. COST — REAL NUMBERS 76% of consumers expect response < 24h. Industry avg: 10+ hours. (Sprout Social 2024) 30–50% silent scraper failure under load. (Apify benchmarks · Bright Data 2025) 02 SOLUTION A boring, observable ingest layer that compounds. Go-based distributed worker pool. Per-platform rate-limit policies. SHA-256 content-hash dedup. Unified Madison schema. Redis Streams bus → consumer groups. WHY MADISON vs. Apify/Bright Data → canonical, deduped. vs. per-agent scrapers → 1 reliable > 5 fragile. vs. Twitter Enterprise → unaffordable + partial. Stagehand fallback when anti-bot escalates. US-01 · DOWNSTREAM AGENT As a Madison Intelligence agent, I want one normalized stream across X / Reddit / LinkedIn, so I don't write per-platform dedup or retry logic. US-02 · ANALYST As a marketing analyst, I want mentions within 5 minutes of posting, so brand teams can respond before the conversation moves. US-03 · OPERATOR As the platform owner, I want per-upstream backpressure visibility, so I see rate-limit pressure before workers silently drop jobs. 04 SUCCESS METRICS METRIC TODAY TARGET MEASURED BY End-to-end freshness 1 – 24 h < 5 min (p95) Event payload ts diff Per-job success rate 50 – 70% (silent) > 99.5% (loud) n8n execution log Schema drift escapes unmeasured 0 Code node reject log New platform onboarding 1 – 2 weeks ad-hoc < 2 days Plan-doc benchmark Monthly infra cost n/a (no system) < $20 / month n8n + Redis Cloud bill DoD: Intelligence agent XREADGROUPs canonical stream · p95 freshness < 5 min over 24-hr soak · 0 schema-drift alerts. 05 EDGE CASES & FAILURE MODES LinkedIn anti-bot (HTTP 999) → Stagehand fallback session; cadence slows 1h. X rate-limit changes mid-day → Hot-reload policy file; no redeploy. Reddit JSON schema drift → Validator rejects + alerts. Never coerce. Slow downstream consumer → Consumer-group lag alert; 7-day trim. Worker crash mid-batch → At-least-once + content-hash dedup at the bus. INFO7375 · BRANDING & AI · ASSIGNMENT 02 · PART 4 — ARCHITECTURE 3 agents. One MVP slice. How it actually runs. Then how to prove it in Week 3. AGENT 01 scraper-worker Pulls one platform on a tick. Rate-limit + exp backoff. Emits raw payload + provenance. Stateless. One per (platform). OUT → raw.<platform> AGENT 02 normalizer Validates Madison schema. Dedups by content_hash. Drops + alerts on drift. Never silently coerces. OUT → canonical.social AGENT 03 distributor Owns Redis Streams + groups. Lag + offset per consumer. 7-day trim. Bounded memory. Backpressure stays visible. OUT → Intelligence · Content · Research PIPELINE FLOW Stateless workers → schema-validated bus → consumer-group fanout. SOURCES X · Twitter Reddit LinkedIn ▶ 01 · scraper-worker rate-limit retry provenance ▶ raw.<platform> 02 · normalizer schema validate content_hash drop on drift ▶ canonical.social 03 · distributor Redis Streams consumer groups lag · trim ▶ DOWNSTREAM Intelligence Content Research PRINCIPLE — never silently coerce. Schema drift = reject + alert. Worker crash = at-least-once + dedup at the bus. STACK Go · errgroup · context.Context · Redis Streams · OpenTelemetry · Grafana Cloud (stretch) · Browserbase Stagehand MVP SCOPE · WEEK 3 reliable-scrape-reddit-mvp One vertical slice. 24-hr soak proves the contract. 01 Schedule Trigger Every 60 seconds 02 HTTP Request Fetch new posts · handle rate limits 03 Code (JavaScript) Clean payload + fingerprint 04 Redis Skip duplicates · push to bus 05 HTTP Request Send to backend · idempotent FLOW: tick → fetch → dedup → send WHAT I WON'T BUILD IN WEEK 3 Honest scope cuts. MVP proves the contract — scaling characteristics come after. ✗ X / LinkedIn scrapers anti-bot too heavy → Week 5 (Stagehand) ✗ Distributed autoscaling single n8n instance → K8s in Week 6 ✗ Multi-region failover single AZ for v0 ✗ Production OTel / Grafana n8n exec logs prove the slice ✗ Hot-reload rate policies env-var config for v0 ✗ Real downstream agents mock /v1/ingest in Week 3 EVENT SCHEMA · BETWEEN AGENTS The contract every agent reads + writes. id unique post id platform reddit · x · linkedin text the post body url link to the post posted_at when the user posted content_hash dedup fingerprint engagement upvotes · likes · etc. Drift here = pipeline broken. Reject + alert.
